checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:146-155`, `src/shared/hooks/useSlidingPane.ts:244-278`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:420,447`. The plan keeps the right portal fix, but it does not absorb the critique correction that the locked-pane failure is driven by `pane.transformClass` moving the entire editor pane to `-translate-y-full`/`translate-y-full`, not just by a generic transformed ancestor creating a fixed-position containing block.

Checked `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:24-28` and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/generation.ts:22-32`. The task idea explicitly includes the create-task tool, but the plan's dedupe step is framed around `generation.ts` and `llm/messages.ts`, so it misses the actual duplicated `asTrimmedString` and `asPositiveNumber` helpers that currently live in `create-task.ts` and `generation.ts`.

Checked `src/app/providers/AppProviders.tsx:33-49`, `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.tsx:87-182`, and `src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx:58-82`. The gallery selection provider is mounted once at app-provider level and the selected ID set is memoized from a stable map, so the user note about the ring flashing on hover is consistent with the plan's CSS-first diagnosis being the most likely first fix.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Checked `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx:352-353`. The plan's proposed replacement class `transition-[shadow,opacity,transform]` is not targeting the actual CSS property Tailwind is animating here, which is `box-shadow`; as written, that fix is underspecified and may not stop the ring/shadow interaction it is trying to isolate.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts:32-92`, `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/command-parser.ts:1`, and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/llm/messages.ts:1-9`. The plan says client and edge validation utilities cannot be shared because of different runtimes, but this repository already shares TypeScript modules across that boundary, so that assumption is technically too strong and leaves avoidable duplication in place.

Checked the three track-creation branches in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:214-223`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:237-246`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:299-309`. The proposed helper name `createVisualTrackAtTop()` does not fit the third caller, which creates either `A*` or `V*` tracks based on the dropped file type, so that extraction would not correctly cover all current behavior without a kind parameter.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:100-499` against its callers in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:420,447`. Portaling inside `AgentChat` is technically sound here because the component only needs React state/context and the repo already uses document-body portals for overlay UI elsewhere.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Checked adjacent tests and related selection code in `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/__tests__/MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx`, `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx`. The plan's named work items focus on only part of the affected surface even though these files already encode related selection propagation, send-pipeline, and chat behavior that would also be at risk.

Checked the broader gallery-selection flow in `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.tsx`, `src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx`, and `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/MediaGalleryGrid.tsx`. The selection state is global and not page-local, so the reported ring bug does not currently look like a broader provider-remount or pagination-reset problem.

Checked the edge-tool scope in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts`, `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/generation.ts`, and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts`. Because the original idea explicitly includes the create-task tool and the loop dispatches into it, a refactor plan that treats only `generation.ts` as the server-side helper cleanup target is narrower than the actual subsystem boundary.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?

findings.detail: Checked existing direct tests for the touched areas. The repository already has `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/__tests__/MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx`, and `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx`, but the plan's validation list does not include them.

Checked `config/testing/vitest.config.ts:13-20`. The plan's final gate of `npx vitest run` will not cover any edge-function work because the default config includes only `src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}` and explicitly excludes `supabase/functions/**`, so the proposed verification does not actually touch `ai-timeline-agent` changes.

Checked the `AgentChat` render sites in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:420,447`. Because both layout branches render the same component and only one branch is active at a time, centralizing the portal change in `AgentChat.tsx` does cover both call sites without extra glue code.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?

findings.detail: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:379-454`. `handleAssetDrop` is called with materially different argument shapes across multi-generation, single-generation, file-upload, and asset-key paths, including `trackId` intentionally being `undefined` to force new-track creation and `insertAtTop` only being forwarded for certain cases, so the dispatcher refactor has to preserve those caller-specific semantics exactly.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:420,447` and `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:153-155`. Both `AgentChat` callers are inside `VideoEditorProvider`, and the two render sites are mutually exclusive layout branches, so a portal inside `AgentChat` should still resolve provider-dependent hooks without double-mounting.

Checked the send pipeline caller contract in `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:241-259` and its existing assertion in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx:43-84`. Refactoring `useAgentSession.ts` without explicitly keeping that caller contract in the verification plan risks silently changing how `generation_id` is serialized for selected attachments.

checks.question: Does the approach match how the codebase solves similar problems?

findings.detail: Checked portal conventions in `src/features/tasks/components/TasksPane/TasksPane.tsx:374-411` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:86-147`. Similar overlay UI in this repo uses `createPortal(..., document.body)` with a `typeof document` guard, but the plan's portal step does not mention following that pattern.

Checked the repo's refactoring guide in `docs/structure_detail/refactoring_patterns.md`. The guide says components under roughly 600 lines should not be split on size alone and that line count is not the signal, while `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` is 499 lines, so Step 10 needs a stronger responsibility-based justification than the current size-driven framing.

Checked the same guide's tradeoff section against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts`. Keeping extracted handlers in the same file is consistent with the repo's guidance for setup-heavy logic that still shares one cohesive ownership boundary.

checks.question: Is there convincing verification for the change?

findings.detail: Checked the existing test surface in `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/__tests__/MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx`, and `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx`. The plan omits all of these targeted tests even though they exercise the exact files the refactor intends to touch.

Checked `docs/structure_detail/refactoring_patterns.md:214`. The repo's own refactoring discipline explicitly requires running `tsc --noEmit` after each extraction, but the plan only schedules Vitest runs at the end, which is weaker than the documented standard for this codebase.

Checked `config/testing/vitest.config.ts:13-20` and `config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts:8-44`. The proposed `npx vitest run` command will miss all edge-function code, and the edge-unit config does not even include `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/**`, so the plan does not currently provide a convincing automated verification path for `loop.ts`, `command-parser.ts`, or `tools/create-task.ts`.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx:49-162`. There is an existing chat test, but it only covers attachment merging and send behavior; it does not assert portal rendering or locked-pane visibility, so the plan is still relying on manual smoke testing for the new pane-positioning behavior.

checks.question: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?

findings.detail: Checked the repository's current test inventory. The `must` criterion saying 'All 5 existing test files pass' is under-scoped because there are more existing relevant tests than the five named in the plan, so it is not a complete yes/no gate for this work.

Checked the two user-visible `must` goals for the selection ring and locked-pane chat. They describe the right product outcomes, but they are mostly manual UI behaviors rather than code-review-verifiable gates, so they are not fully testable from the listed automated checks alone.

Checked the numeric refactor goals in the plan against `docs/structure_detail/refactoring_patterns.md`. The file-length and nesting targets are correctly kept in `should` rather than `must`, which matches the repo's guidance that structure matters more than raw size.

flags.concern: agent-chat-rendering: Plan analysis still frames the locked-pane bug around a generic transformed ancestor and does not explicitly account for `pane.transformClass` sliding the whole editor pane off-screen.
flags.evidence: `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:146-149` applies `pane.transformClass`, and `src/shared/hooks/useSlidingPane.ts:244-259` returns `-translate-y-full`/`translate-y-full` for hidden top/bottom panes.

flags.concern: agent-tool-utils: Deduplication scope misses the actual shared helpers in `tools/create-task.ts` and assumes client/server sharing is impossible even though the repo already shares cross-runtime modules.
flags.evidence: `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:24-28` and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/generation.ts:22-32` both define `asTrimmedString` and `asPositiveNumber`, while edge files already import from `src/...` in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/command-parser.ts:1` and `llm/messages.ts:9`.

flags.concern: verification: Planned validation omits existing direct tests for the touched frontend files and the final `npx vitest run` command excludes `supabase/functions/**`, leaving the edge refactor largely unverified.
drop-handling: The proposed `createVisualTrackAtTop` extraction does not match all current callers because file drops create both audio and visual tracks depending on file type.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:214-223` and `:237-246` hardcode `V*` tracks, but `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:299-309` derives `A*` vs `V*` from `kind`.

flags.concern: agent-chat-refactor-threshold: Step 10 proposes splitting `AgentChat.tsx` largely on line count even though the repo's own refactoring guide says sub-600-line components should not be split on size alone.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` is 499 lines (`wc -l`), and `docs/structure_detail/refactoring_patterns.md` says the component threshold is `> 600` and that 'Line count alone is not the signal.'
